text: Should Philadelphia be allowed to pass its own taxes to pay for SEPTA?
description: Currently, Philly must ask Harrisburg for money. This would let the city raise its own transit funds.
whyItMatters: This determines if SEPTA cuts your bus route or keeps it running.

text: Should the lowest pay in Pennsylvania be raised to $15 an hour right now?
description: PA's minimum wage is $7.25. This doubles it.
whyItMatters: Directly impacts the paycheck of Philly's service and retail workers.

text: Should high-earners pay more taxes to keep Social Security fully funded?
description: Raises the tax cap for people making over $400k a year.
whyItMatters: Determines the future stability of your retirement checks.

text: Should Congress pass a federal law protecting voting access regardless of state laws?
description: Sets national standards for mail-in and early voting.
whyItMatters: Affects how easy it is to cast your vote for Congress or President.

text: Should the federal government ban 'assault-style' weapons nationwide?
description: Stops the sale of semi-automatic firearms nationwide.
whyItMatters: The central federal debate on reducing gun violence in Philly.

text: Should the U.S. provide tax breaks for electric cars and solar panels?
description: Uses tax money to make green energy cheaper for homeowners.
whyItMatters: Lowers your energy bills and shifts the country away from oil.

text: Should the state allow Philadelphia to pass laws that limit how much landlords can increase rent each year?
description: Right now, only the state can decide rent rules. This would give Home Rule to Philly to control rent costs.
whyItMatters: This directly affects how much your rent could go up next year.

text: Should the state increase funding for the 'Whole-Home Repairs' program to help low-income homeowners fix roofs and windows?
description: This uses state grants to help people stay in their homes by fixing major safety issues.
whyItMatters: It helps prevent blight in Philly neighborhoods and keeps long-time residents from being forced out.

text: Should the state significantly increase funding for the Philadelphia School District to comply with the court order on fair funding?
description: PA courts ruled the state funding system is unconstitutional. This would send billions more to underfunded districts.
whyItMatters: This determines if your local school gets more teachers, newer books, and better facilities.

text: Should Pennsylvania provide universal free preschool (Pre-K) for every 4-year-old in the state?
description: Uses state tax money to ensure every child can start school early regardless of family income.
whyItMatters: This would save Philly parents thousands of dollars in childcare and early education costs.
